title: Design Patterns — Structural
learningObjectives: Defend **Structural design patterns** choices with trade-offs, failure domains, and metrics
Contrast patterns and anti-patterns interviewers probe with follow-up questions
Sketch boundaries, data flows, and consistency models on a whiteboard
Relate scalability, cost, and operational complexity to team constraints
Use structured responses: requirements, options, decision, verification
Prepare STAR-style stories where architecture decisions had measurable impact

sections.title: Why Design Patterns — Structural matters
sections.content: **Structural** patterns answer "**how** **pieces** **fit**". Interviews probe **Decorator** vs **Proxy** and **Adapter** at **integration** boundaries.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Design Patterns — Structural
sections.content:
### Day 79 — **Structural** **patterns**

### 1. Decorator
**Stack** **behavior** without **class** **explosion**.

### 2. Proxy
**Control** **access**, **lazy** **load**, **guards**.

### 3. Adapter
**Legacy** **API** **fit** **new** **port**.

### 4. Facade
**Reduce** **coupling** to **subsystem**.

### 5. Composite
**Tree** **structures** **uniformly**.

### 6. Story
**Caching** **decorator** around **repository**—**transparent** **read** **through**.

sections.title: Basic — Structural trio
sections.output: decorator proxy adapter facade bridge composite

sections.title: Intermediate — Decorator stack
Advanced — Proxy control
Decorator object chain
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 79 — Decorator
component A as Core
component B as LogDec
component C as MetricDec
C --> B
B --> A
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Design Patterns — Structural (Day 79 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly** one line: decorator: add behavior; proxy: control access

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **Decorator** vs **subclassing**?
conceptual.answer: Compose behavior at runtime stacking wrappers; subclassing multiplies classes for combinations. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Structural design patterns**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Compose behavior at runtime stacking wrappers; subclassing multiplies classes for combinations. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Compose behavior at runtime stacking wrappers; subclassing multiplies classes for combinat Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

conceptual.question: **Proxy** **types** interview list?
conceptual.answer: Remote, virtual, protection—control access, lazy init, security checks. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Structural design patterns**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Remote, virtual, protection—control access, lazy init, security checks. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.question: **Bridge** decouples **what** from **how**?
conceptual.answer: Abstraction from implementation so both vary independently. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Design Patterns — Structural** deploy correlates with **5xx** spike.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze risky changes; open **SLO** dashboard.

**(2) Root cause** **Config** regression or **dependency** **saturation**.

**(3) Fix** Rollback or **toggle** **flag**; restore **SLO** then root-cause.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.answer: Share **SLO** **burn**, **customer** **regions** affected, **mitigation** steps in flight, and **explicit** unknowns. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Pattern | Recall |
|---|---|
| Decorator | Add behavior |
| Proxy | Control access |
| Adapter | Interface map |
| Facade | Simplify API |
| Bridge | Abstraction split |
| Composite | Tree uniform |
| Flyweight | Shared state |
| JDK Proxy | Dynamic |
| Delegate | Composition |
| Anti | Confuse intent |
